title: Force Push Recovery via Reflog
description: A teammate force-pushed to `origin/main` and overwrote three commits that contained critical work. Your local repo still has those commits in its reflog. Recover the lost commits and restore the remote.
objective: The branch `main` on both local and `origin` must contain the three lost commits. The commit history must be linear. You may need to use `--force-with-lease` or `--force` to push the restored history back to origin.
hints: `git reflog` on your local repo will show the SHAs of commits that existed before the force push.
Find the last good SHA and use `git reset --hard <sha>` to restore your local branch.
`git push --force-with-lease origin main` updates the remote with your restored history.
